rulings.Case Title: RONALD C. SILBERSTEIN VS. HAROLD BRANDEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 29, 2019, line 1. PETITIONER RONALD SILBERSTEIN'S Motion On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award. Ronald Silberstein petitions "to vacate the Corrected Interim Arbitration Award issued on November 19, 2018." However, courts may review only final arbitration awards, which this award is not. (Maplebear, Inc. v. Busick (2018) 26 Cal.App.5th 394, 407; CCP §1283.4.) The petition is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD KATZMAN VS. G. WHITNEY LEIGH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Show Cause Re: Order Of Contempt By Non-Party And Further Relief, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Supporting Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 29, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF RICHARD KATZMAN's Motion For Order To Show Cause Re: Order Of Contempt By Non-Party And Further Relief. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Motion is granted in part. Third party witness Kevin Sullivan shall attend a deposition and pay $750.80 in sanctions on April 22, 2019 or another date agreed upon with Plaintiff. The motion for contempt is denied without prejudice to it being re-filed if Mr. Sullivan does not comply with this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL DISANTO VS. COTCHETT, PITRE & MCCARTHY, LLP. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff To Lift The Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 29, 2019, line 10. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL DISANTO'S Motion To Lift The Stay. Plaintiff Michael DiSanto's motion to lift stay is denied. The arbitration provision in plaintiff's retainer agreement with defendant law firm states that "any dispute" relating to that agreement "shall be submitted to JAMS in San Francisco, CA, or if that forum is not available, to another equivalent alternative dispute resolution forum." A "forum" is "a court or arbitral tribunal - each with its own set of rules and procedures," and each ADR provider has its own set of rules and procedures. (Alan v. Sup. Ct. (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 217, 224.) JAMS having disqualified itself, the dispute "shall be submitted" to an alternative ADR provider. Plaintiff's cited cases do not help him. Unlike the agreement in Martinez v. Master Protection Corporation (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 107, the arbitration agreement here does not mandate one exclusive arbitral forum, but instead provides for an "equivalent" ADR alternative if JAMS is unavailable. Moreover, contracts are not interpreted against their drafters when, as here, they are unambiguous. (Badie v. Bank of America (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 779, 801.) The parties are ordered to meet and confer to select an ADR provider for their arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ERIC HALLIMEN VS. BIG 3 ENTERTAINMENT INC. DBA THE LISTER BAR A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 29, 2019, line 7. DEFENDANT BIG 3 ENTERTAINMENT, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant bar owner Big 3 Entertainment, Inc. moves for summary judgment on one issue: did it owe a legal duty to a bar patron assaulted by other patrons? This case is similar to Delgado v. Trax Bar & Grill (2005) 36 Cal.4th 224. However, Delgado is less favorable to defendant, as it involved an assault outside a bar, while the assault here was inside the premises. (Id. at 231; Hallimen Dec. 2-9.) Delgado holds that a bar "has a duty to respond to events unfolding in" a bouncer's "presence by undertaking reasonable, relatively simple, and minimally burdensome measures." (36 Cal.4th at 245-46.) In our case, plaintiff declares that defendant's bouncer saw the assault unfold but took no action. (Hallimen Dec. 8-9.) This mixed question of fact and law leaves a disputed issue for trial. The summary judgment motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD BRANDEL ET AL VS. 661 HOWARD STREET LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In 661 Howard Streets Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 29, 2019, line 8. DEFENDANT THIRSTY BEAR BREWING COMPANY LLC'S Joinder In 661 Howard Street's Motion To Compel Arbitration. Defendant (and landlord) 661 Howard Street LLC's motion to compel arbitration is granted. The subject lease's arbitration language - "[a]ny controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this" lease - is quite broad, and California's policy favoring arbitration is quite strong. (Khalatian v. Prime Time Shuttle, Inc. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-60; Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 9.) Plaintiffs' argument that their complaint is not related to the lease is belied by the complaint's first paragraph; it repeats the word "lease" six times, pleading, e.g., that "lease payments¿substantially exceed the Landlord's direct costs of ownership." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
